# Data submission with IOTC forms  
The  ([IOTC ROS forms](https://data.iotc.org/reference/latest/forms/#Regional_Observer_Scheme_Datasets)). are the mandatory format for reporting ROS data. Within the reporting forms, data fields are visually differentiated by colour to indicate the requirements condition of each field where fields in blue are mandatory for reporting, and green fields are optional. Completion of optional fields is encouraged when feasible. 

## IOTC ROS Data form structure  
The ROS forms are XLSX workbooks comprising several sheets that varies according to the reporting main gear. The IOTC form organises data into hierarchical levels (Fig 1) with a general structure for all the gear that includes: 

### METADATA
The first worksheet in the ROS FORM (META) Include information of the data submission.  

Then, the IOTC form organises data into hierarchical levels, whit the observed trip as the sampling unit.    


### OBSERVER INFORMATION (O-INFO) 
Following the identification of an observed trip, data in this section includes the identification of the observer with their respective IOTC ID, as well the duration of their time onboard, and a summary of the activities conducted while on board.  


### VESSEL INFORMATION (V-SHEETS) 
Includes the identification of the vessel (V-INFO), the duration of the vessel’s trip (V-TRIP), which may differ from the observer trip and the main vessel attributes data (V-ATTRIBUTES) including information on onboard electronics, fish storage and preservation methods, and waste management capacity. 

### GEAR INFORMATION (G-SHEETS) 
This section focusses on the main fishing gear, including general and specific configurations and/or mitigation devices (e.g. tori lines on longline vessels, or mesh size data for the net aboard a purse seine vessel).  

### OPERATIONS INFORMATION (E-SHEETS) 
Operations are referring in the form as sets. Setting operations (E-SET sections) are first identified with start time and location for each observed set.  Gear data reported in this section identifies gear configuration used for each individual set.  The reporting form sections describing setting operations have multiple subsections. The data in these sections may also include reporting bait used, sighting cues, mitigation devices, and hauling operations depending on the gear.  

Biological set data (E-SET-CATCHES) are associated with a specific Catch ID and summarizes the total weight of catch by species and fate. The following sheets go into more detailed information from the total catch to individual specimens. Within a specific Catch ID, each individual sampled is assigned a specimen ID (E-SET-CATCHES-SPECIMEN) allowing the reporting of length and weight measurements for the individuals, maturity, and sex. Other fields are also including information of the collection of biological samples that may be taken for scientific projects approved by the Scientific Committee.  

Additional information is requested for  ([Species of Special Interest (SSIs)](https://data.iotc.org/reference/latest/domain/biology/#SSIspecies)) in the E-SET-CATCHES-SPECIMEN-SSI section. 


```{r PLStructure, fig.cap = "Structure of the Pole-and-Line reporting forms. Each sheet contains a unique identifier (UI) to identify the trip. Subsetions, each containing individual fields are mapped. Mandatory subsections are shown in blue, while optional are shown in green.", fig.asp = 0.75, width = 8}
knitr::include_graphics("../../inputs/charts/IOTC_ROS_PL.png")
```


Specific unique identifiers (Table 1) allow the association and connection of data across hierarchical levels (e.g. an individual specimen to be linked to a unique catch sample, for a specific set, on an observed trip).  


Table 1. Identification and descriptions of each of the levels/resolutions of data required in the reporting forms. 


| Data Level | Identifier | Description |
| :--------| :----: | :----------------------- | 
| Observed Trip | Observed Trip ID | An observed trip is defined as the period during which an observer is assigned on board a vessel during a specific fishing trip, operationally defined by the vessel’s activities between a departure from port and the next arrival involving a landing, transhipment or partial/complete cessation of operations.  |
| Set/Event | Set ID / Event ID | Within a trip, there are individual operations (sets) conducted. On pole-and-line vessels, there are individual events. |
| Catch | Catch ID | A subset of the catch from a set that is sampled.   |
| Specimen | Specimen ID | An individual for which data is recorded during fishing operations, either as a discard or sampled as part of the retained catch.  | 

Are some requirements specific for some gears and species. Some examples are described below, nothing that those can vary in relation with the evolution of the CMMs implemented by the IOTC.
